channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump team says Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire deal brokered by Biden is actually Trump's win
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration kept President-elect Donald Trump's incoming administration closely apprised of its efforts to broker the ceasefire deal between Israel and Hezbollah that took effect early Wednesday, according to the outgoing Democratic administration.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump fills out his economic team with two veterans of his first administration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is turning to two officials with experience navigating not only Washington but the key issues of income taxes and tariffs as he fills out his economic team.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein And Josh Boak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump taps Jamieson Greer for US trade representative, announces more picks
item.description: President-elect Trump announced several appointments and nominations Tuesday night of those he wishes to serve in his next administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico suggests it would impose its own tariffs to retaliate against any Trump tariffs
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — President Claudia Sheinbaum suggested Tuesday that Mexico could retaliate with tariffs of its own, after U.S. President-elect Donald Trump threatened to impose 25% import duties on Mexican goods if the country doesn’t stop the flow of drugs and migrants across the border.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: After delay, Trump signs agreement with Biden White House to begin formal transition handoff
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday reached a required agreement with President Joe Biden’s White House to allow his transition staff to coordinate with the existing federal workforce before taking office on Jan. 20.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris' campaign leaders say there was a 'price to be paid' for shortened campaign against Trump
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The leaders of Kamala Harris' presidential campaign insist they simply didn't have enough time to execute a winning strategy against Donald Trump, pointing to “ferocious" political headwinds that were ultimately too much to overcome in the 107-day period after President Joe Biden ste
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden administration seeking to finalize $6.6B loan to build EV factory before term ends
item.description: The Department of Energy (DOE) is working to finalize a $6 billion loan to fund construction for an electric vehicle facility in Georgia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lawmakers call for progress on marijuana law before end of Biden administration
item.description: Progressive lawmakers are calling on President Biden to take additional steps on federal marijuana law before his term comes to an end in January. In a letter sent to Biden and Vice President Harris, Democratic Reps. Barbara Lee (Calif.), Earl Blumenauer (Ore.) and Ilhan Omar (Minn.) and Sens.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Surveillance tech advances by Biden could aid in Trump's promised crackdown on immigration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump will return to power next year with a raft of technological tools at his disposal that would help deliver his campaign promise of cracking down on immigration — among them, surveillance and artificial intelligence technology that the Biden administration already uses to
item.dc:creator: Sarah Parvini, Garance Burke And Jesse Bedayn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: California Bay Area city considers ordinance blocking local resources from supporting Trump mass deportations
item.description: Redwood City's city council voted in favor of having staff draft an ordinance for future consideration that would restrict the city from cooperating with immigration authorities
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas offers Trump administration more land and support for mass deportations
item.description: Two events in the Rio Grande Valley on Tuesday made clear Texas officials will work with the Trump administration on immigration.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Berenice Garcia, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden officials, Republicans point fingers over exhausted disaster loans program
item.description: A blame game between the Biden administration and GOP lawmakers is underway after a key disaster loan program saw its funds run out in the middle of a destructive hurricane season.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Lauren Boebert Cameo page disappears amid House ethics concerns
item.description: Rep. Lauren Boebert (R-Colo.) briefly offered videos on Cameo, a website on which public figures sell personalized videos to fans, before the page went offline amid questions about whether it would have violated House ethics rules.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Here's where Trump's criminal cases stand
item.description: President-elect Trump’s legal strategy of delay is paying off, with his election victory changing the trajectory of his four criminal prosecutions.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's favorability climbs in post-election poll
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump’s favorability climbed by six points after he was elected president earlier this month, a new poll on Tuesday found.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's hush money case in limbo after judge adjourns sentencing
item.description: New York Judge Juan Merchan faces a choice of freezing President-elect Trump's hush money case in place until he leaves the White House or tossing it entirely, underscoring how Trump's election win has changed the dynamic of his legal travails.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Post-election poll: Trump victory, cabinet choices, war, and aliens through voters' eyes
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump's popularity is on the rise and the voters who went against him think World War III is likely.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump has promised lower taxes. These are 3 ways it could impact you
item.description: From "no tax on tips" to Social Security, Donald Trump has made a range of promises about the taxes Americans will pay. We take a look at three changes we could see in the months ahead.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Wamsley, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota DNR delays decision on key proposed copper-nickel mine permit
item.description: The Minnesota Department of Natural Resources has delayed a decision until late next year on a key permit for the proposed NewRange Copper Nickel project in northeastern Minnesota, the mine formerly known as PolyMet. The company called the agency’s decision “unfortunate.
item.dc:creator: Dan Kraker, MPR News

item.title: Trump expected to name investor Scott Bessent as Treasury secretary pick
item.description: Bessent, founder and CEO of hedge fund Key Square Group, served as an economic adviser to the Trump campaign, often appearing on financial talk shows to argue in favor of the president-elect's economic policies.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, Fox 5

item.title: Republican who lost Minnesota House race sues claiming DFL winner lives outside district
item.description: The Roseville-area court challenge is the latest legal action Republicans are using to try and break the tie in the Minnesota House next year and muster up a majority in one chamber.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: President-elect set to shape ’Trump judiciary’ for next generation
item.description: Legal experts predict President-elect Donald Trump will move quickly next year to cement and extend his legacy in appointing federal judges.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Johnson, MPR News

item.title: How a Northfield farmer picks which of his turkeys to have pardoned at White House
item.description: The competition to meet the president started months ago when John Zimmerman, his son and neighbors began examining 44 turkeys from his flock for the Thanksgiving honor.
item.dc:creator: Todd Melby, MPR News

item.title: Trump chooses Pam Bondi for attorney general pick after Gaetz withdraws
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Thursday named Pam Bondi, the former attorney general of Florida, to be U.S. attorney general just hours after his other choice, Matt Gaetz, withdrew his name.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Report: Minnesota Lottery needs to tighten up how it regulates retailers
item.description: Minnesota’s Office of the Legislative Auditor reports that the Minnesota State Lottery generally complies with requirements, but has room to improve when it comes to how it manages retailers.
item.dc:creator: Anika Besst, MPR News

item.title: Hennepin County looks to spread the word of Minnesota’s red flag law
item.description: Nearly a year after Minnesota’s red flag law took effect, the Hennepin County Attorney’s Office says it’s looking to expand information and training on how it works.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Musk, Ramaswamy lay out plans for 'mass' federal layoffs, rule rollbacks under Trump
item.description: Tech entrepreneurs Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy said Wednesday that their brand-new government efficiency panel will identify “thousands” of regulations for President-elect Donald Trump to eliminate, which they argue will justify “mass head-count reductions” across government.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, WFLA, NBC
